evals.prompt: What is the current price of BTC/TWD on BitoPro?
evals.expected_output: The agent constructs a GET request to /tickers/btc_twd and returns the latest price data.
expectations: The agent uses the get_tickers tool
The request URL includes /tickers/btc_twd
The trading pair is lowercase with underscore: btc_twd
No authentication headers are included (public endpoint)

evals.prompt: Show me the order book for ETH/TWD with 10 levels of depth.
evals.expected_output: The agent constructs a GET request to /order-book/eth_twd with limit=10.
expectations: The agent uses the get_order_book tool
The request URL includes /order-book/eth_twd
The limit parameter is set to 10
The trading pair is lowercase with underscore: eth_twd

evals.prompt: Get hourly candlestick data for BTC/TWD from the last 24 hours.
evals.expected_output: The agent constructs a GET request to /trading-history/btc_twd with resolution=1h and appropriate from/to timestamps in seconds.
expectations: The agent uses the get_candlesticks tool
The request URL includes /trading-history/btc_twd
The resolution parameter is set to 1h
The from and to parameters are Unix timestamps in seconds (not milliseconds)

evals.prompt: Check my account balance on BitoPro.
evals.expected_output: The agent constructs an authenticated GET request to /accounts/balance with proper HMAC-SHA384 signing.
expectations: The agent uses the get_account_balance tool
The agent mentions that BITOPRO_API_KEY, BITOPRO_API_SECRET, and BITOPRO_EMAIL are required
The payload includes identity (email) and nonce fields
The signing algorithm is HMAC-SHA384
Three headers are included: X-BITOPRO-APIKEY, X-BITOPRO-PAYLOAD, X-BITOPRO-SIGNATURE

evals.prompt: Place a limit buy order for 0.001 BTC at 2,800,000 TWD on BitoPro.
evals.expected_output: The agent asks for user confirmation before placing the order, then constructs an authenticated POST request to /orders/btc_twd.
expectations: The agent confirms the order details with the user before executing
The agent uses the create_order tool
The request is a POST to /orders/btc_twd
The signing payload includes request body parameters plus nonce, but NOT identity
The X-BITOPRO-PAYLOAD header is included for POST requests

evals.prompt: Cancel order 1234567890 for BTC/TWD on BitoPro.
evals.expected_output: The agent constructs an authenticated DELETE request to /orders/btc_twd/1234567890 with all three auth headers.
expectations: The agent uses the cancel_order tool
The request is a DELETE to /orders/btc_twd/1234567890
The signing payload uses identity + nonce (same as GET)
All three auth headers are included: X-BITOPRO-APIKEY, X-BITOPRO-PAYLOAD, X-BITOPRO-SIGNATURE
